stories.title: Stuttgart BOSS Open Final — Fritz vs Shelton, All-American Showdown
stories.angle: Historic all-American ATP final — first time two Americans have met in an ATP final in 71 years. Defending champion Taylor Fritz (beat Bublik 6-4, 6-4 in SF) faces Ben Shelton (saved 2 match points vs Lehecka 6-7(4), 7-6(14), 7-6(6) in SF). Shelton leads H2H 2-1, including 2026 Dallas Open final win. Both players entering Wimbledon-prep form. Final scheduled post 2:00 PM local time in Stuttgart.

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text: Fritz (defending champ) vs Shelton — All-American Stuttgart final. First time two Americans have met in an ATP final in 71 years. Wimbledon is 15 days away. ⚡ #Tennis #BossOpen #Shelton #Fritz

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text: Ben Shelton saved 2 match points in a 7-6(14) tiebreak vs Lehecka to reach the Stuttgart final. No. 1 seed. Nerves of steel. Now he faces defending champ Fritz for the title. 🎾 #Tennis #BossOpen #Shelton

article.headline: Fritz vs Shelton: All-American Stuttgart Final Is First in 71 Years

stories.title: Queen's Club WTA Final — Raducanu Chases First British Women's Title in 56 Years
stories.angle: Emma Raducanu (world No. 42) vs Donna Vekic (lucky loser, world No. 19) in the HSBC Championships final at Queen's Club. Raducanu played double duty Saturday — beat Rakhimova in QF, then Jovic 6-2, 6-2 in SF — without dropping a set all week. Vekic dominant, beat Boulter 6-1, 6-3 in SF. First career meeting. Raducanu would be first British woman to win Queen's since Winnie Shaw in 1970 (56 years). Wimbledon starts June 29.

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text: Emma Raducanu played TWO matches Saturday at Queen's Club and didn't drop a set. Now she's in Sunday's final vs Vekic. First British woman in the Queen's Club final since 1970. 🇬🇧 #Tennis #QueensClub #Raducanu

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text: Raducanu vs Vekic — Queen's Club WTA final. Win would be Raducanu's first title since the 2021 US Open. First British woman to win Queen's since Winnie Shaw in 1970. 56 years in the making. 🎾 #Tennis #QueensClub #Raducanu

article.headline: Raducanu vs Vekic: Queen's Club Final Could Make British Tennis History

stories.title: Libema Open Final — Majchrzak's Cinderella Run Meets de Minaur
stories.angle: Kamil Majchrzak (Poland) faces Alex de Minaur (Australia, world No. 6, defending 2024 champion) in the Libema Open final at 's-Hertogenbosch. Majchrzak reached his first ATP final with back-to-back upsets of FAA (world No. 4) in QF and Medvedev (No. 3 seed) 7-6(4), 6-1 in SF. De Minaur rallied past Mannarino in SF. Final at 2:30 PM local. If Majchrzak wins, first ATP title for him.

x_posts.label: Post 3-1
x_posts.text: Kamil Majchrzak's week at 's-Hertogenbosch: def. No. 4 Auger-Aliassime in QF, def. Medvedev in SF, now faces de Minaur in the FINAL. First ATP final of his career. 🎾 #Tennis #LibemaOpen #Majchrzak

x_posts.label: Post 3-2
x_posts.text: Alex de Minaur rallied from a set down to beat Mannarino and reach the Libema Open final. Now the 2024 defending champion faces Majchrzak for a second 's-Hertogenbosch title. #Tennis #LibemaOpen #deMinaur

stories.title: Wimbledon 2026 — 15 Days Out, Contenders Come Into Focus
stories.angle: Wimbledon begins June 29; draw set for June 26; qualifying June 22-25. Sinner (No. 1, defending champion) has skipped all grass warm-ups except Hurlingham exhibition June 23-27. Alcaraz (No. 2) confirmed out with wrist injury. Today's stuttgarts and Queen's finalists give clearest form guides ahead of Wimbledon. Women: Swiatek defending, Andreeva (Roland Garros champion) arrives with no grass points to defend. Rybakina fell in QF.

x_posts.label: Post 4
x_posts.text: Wimbledon starts June 29. Alcaraz is out (wrist). Sinner (defending) skipped all warm-ups. Raducanu is in a final TODAY. The grass season storylines are writing themselves. Draw: June 26. 🌿 #Tennis #Wimbledon2026

- [HIGH] Mirra Andreeva is 2026 Roland Garros champion (Source: prior pipeline research June 9)
- [HIGH] Elena Rybakina fell in QF at Queen's Club (to Boulter) (Source: wtatennis.com)

stories.title: Triple Finals Sunday — Week 1 of Grass Sets Wimbledon Stage
stories.angle: All three grass-court tournaments conclude on June 14 with simultaneous finals: Stuttgart (all-American Fritz vs Shelton), Libema (Majchrzak vs de Minaur), Queen's Club WTA (Raducanu vs Vekic). Historic day for tennis — first all-American ATP final in 71 years headlines a weekend where Medvedev, Rybakina, FAA, and Boulter all fell to inspired underdogs. Week 1 of grass has rewritten the Wimbledon form book.

x_posts.label: Post 5
x_posts.text: Sunday June 14: three ATP/WTA grass finals, same day. Stuttgart. 's-Hertogenbosch. Queen's Club. Medvedev, Rybakina, and FAA all gone. The Wimbledon form book has been rewritten. 🌿 #Tennis #GrassSeason #Wimbledon2026
